title: install ceremony has no reflected CLI op + enforcement hooks scoped by op-shape not target-substrate (TASK-033 dogfooding)
question: Why can a CLI-driven pi-context bootstrap not complete (no reflected install op), and why do the PreToolUse enforcement hooks fire on writes to non-active/throwaway substrates?
method: code-inspection (installContext + the /context install slash handler + ops-registry + gap-register-guard.sh + block-pi-context-glue.sh + parity-check) + empirical reproduction (a /tmp substrate: init+accept-all leaves schemas/ empty; pi -p "/context install" observed not to dispatch; the guard blocking a --cwd /tmp append) + substrate prior-art search.
scope: reproduction: a /tmp throwaway substrate, init+accept-all then unmaterialized; pi -p prompt-mode non-dispatch of /context install
substrate prior-art: FGAP-030 (CLI-parity precedent), FGAP-074, the block-substrate-cli-bypass.sh spec
findings_summary: A TASK-033 runtime-demo dogfooding pass surfaced two experience gaps, agent-investigated to root cause / shape / repro / class. GAP A (FGAP-088, RESOLVED by TASK-059): the install ceremony had no reflected CLI op — installContext (index.ts:1211) was surfaced only as the /context install slash-command handler (index.ts:3098-3149) and allowlisted in INTENTIONALLY_UNEXPOSED_WRITERS (ops-registry.ts:2172-2175), while its five lifecycle siblings (init/accept-all/switch/list/archive) are reflected ops. A CLI-driven init+accept-all reached a config-complete-but-unmaterialized substrate (schemas/ empty, no block files) with no CLI op to advance it; a subsequent --autoId append threw 'nextId: schema not found' (block-api.ts:2311). There was no non-interactive path to install: pi -p "/context install" does NOT dispatch it (prompt-mode hands the text to an agent that does not slash-dispatch — observed: haiku-4.5 replied the command 'doesn't seem to be a standard command' and materialized nothing), and install is not a registered op/tool. Install was reachable only via an interactive pi slash command. Class: substrate-lifecycle operations unevenly reflected to the CLI (the CLI-parity family FGAP-030 named); install was the last unreflected member of the init -> accept-all -> install bootstrap triad. GAP B (FGAP-089, OPEN — TASK-060): the PreToolUse enforcement hooks scope on op-shape + block-name, never on the target substrate. gap-register-guard.sh (:20-21) matches write-op name + planning-block name/array-key, and block-pi-context-glue.sh (:22) matches the pi-context CLI invocation shape; neither inspects --cwd or the active-substrate pointer (.pi-context.json contextDir), so both fire on writes to throwaway/non-active --cwd substrates outside the convention they protect (it blocked the TASK-033 demo seed). The hook input carries the --cwd text but the hooks do not parse it. Class: enforcement hooks scoped by op-shape rather than target-substrate, firing outside the active substrate they protect — covers both installed Bash-matcher hooks; the not-installed block-substrate-cli-bypass.sh spec is the resource-scoped counter-example. Both gaps were untracked before this investigation; both filed at class altitude (FGAP-088 / FGAP-089), fixes TASK-059 (landed) / TASK-060 (pending).
stale_conditions: GAP-A grounding superseded by TASK-059: the reflected context-install op landed and installContext was removed from INTENTIONALLY_UNEXPOSED_WRITERS — the no-reflected-install-op finding is historical
GAP-B resolved when the hooks are scoped to the active substrate (TASK-060) — the op-shape-not-target-substrate finding becomes historical
pi's --tools / slash-dispatch semantics change (pi-runtime-internal), altering the pi -p non-dispatch finding

citations.label: installContext engine + the /context install slash-command handler
the --autoId append throw on an unmaterialized substrate (nextId before block read)
gap-register-guard.sh — op-name + block-name match, no --cwd inspection
block-pi-context-glue.sh — CLI-invocation-shape match, no --cwd inspection (the confirmed sibling)
